items.verse: ۞ وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَٰهِيمُ لِأَبِيهِ ءَازَرَ أَتَتَّخِذُ أَصْنَامًا ءَالِهَةً ۖ إِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰكَ وَقَوْمَكَ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍۢ مُّبِينٍۢ
items.verseEn: And [mention, O Muḥammad], when Abraham said to his father Āzar, "Do you take idols as deities? Indeed, I see you and your people to be in manifest error."

items.verse: وَهِىَ تَجْرِى بِهِمْ فِى مَوْجٍۢ كَٱلْجِبَالِ وَنَادَىٰ نُوحٌ ٱبْنَهُۥ وَكَانَ فِى مَعْزِلٍۢ يَـٰبُنَىَّ ٱرْكَب مَّعَنَا وَلَا تَكُن مَّعَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ
items.verseEn: And it sailed with them through waves like mountains, and Noah called to his son who was apart [from them], "O my son, come aboard with us and be not with the disbelievers."

items.verse: قَالَ يَـٰبُنَىَّ لَا تَقْصُصْ رُءْيَاكَ عَلَىٰٓ إِخْوَتِكَ فَيَكِيدُوا۟ لَكَ كَيْدًا ۖ إِنَّ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنَ لِلْإِنسَـٰنِ عَدُوٌّۭ مُّبِينٌۭ
items.verseEn: He said, "O my son, do not relate your vision to your brothers or they will contrive against you a plan. Indeed Satan, to man, is a manifest enemy.

items.verse: وَيَـٰٓـَٔادَمُ ٱسْكُنْ أَنتَ وَزَوْجُكَ ٱلْجَنَّةَ فَكُلَا مِنْ حَيْثُ شِئْتُمَا وَلَا تَقْرَبَا هَـٰذِهِ ٱلشَّجَرَةَ فَتَكُونَا مِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ
items.verseEn: And "O Adam, dwell, you and your wife, in Paradise and eat from wherever you will but do not approach this tree, lest you be among the wrongdoers."

categories.name: طَعَامُ أَهْلِ النَّارِ
categories.nameEn: Foods of the People of Hell

items.verseEn: Nor any food except from the discharge of wounds;

items.verse: مِّن وَرَآئِهِۦ جَهَنَّمُ وَيُسْقَىٰ مِن مَّآءٍۢ صَدِيدٍۢ
items.verseEn: Before him is Hell, and he will be given a drink of purulent water.
Its emerging fruit as if it was heads of the devils.
So have you considered al-Lāt and al-ʿUzzā?
And Manāt, the third - the other one?
Do you call upon Baʿl and leave the best of creators -

items.verse: وَقَالُوا۟ لَا تَذَرُنَّ ءَالِهَتَكُمْ وَلَا تَذَرُنَّ وَدًّۭا وَلَا سُوَاعًۭا وَلَا يَغُوثَ وَيَعُوقَ وَنَسْرًۭا
items.verseEn: And said, 'Never leave your gods and never leave Wadd or Suwāʿ or Yaghūth and Yaʿūq and Nasr.'
So he threw it down, and thereupon it was a snake, moving swiftly.

items.verse: قُلْنَا يَـٰنَارُ كُونِى بَرْدًۭا وَسَلَـٰمًا عَلَىٰٓ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ
items.verseEn: We [i.e., Allāh] said, "O fire, be coolness and safety upon Abraham."

items.verse: وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمُ رَبِّ أَرِنِى كَيْفَ تُحْىِ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ ۖ قَالَ أَوَلَمْ تُؤْمِن ۖ قَالَ بَلَىٰ وَلَـٰكِن لِّيَطْمَئِنَّ قَلْبِى ۖ قَالَ فَخُذْ أَرْبَعَةًۭ مِّنَ ٱلطَّيْرِ فَصُرْهُنَّ إِلَيْكَ ثُمَّ ٱجْعَلْ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ جَبَلٍۢ مِّنْهُنَّ جُزْءًۭا ثُمَّ ٱدْعُهُنَّ يَأْتِينَكَ سَعْيًۭا ۚ وَٱعْلَمْ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: And [mention] when Abraham said, "My Lord, show me how You give life to the dead." [Allāh] said, "Have you not believed?" He said, "Yes, but [I ask] only that my heart may be satisfied." [Allāh] said, "Take four birds and commit them to yourself. Then [after slaughtering them] put on each hill a portion of them; then call them - they will come [flying] to you in haste. And know that Allāh is Exalted in Might and Wise."

items.verse: وَإِلَىٰ ثَمُودَ أَخَاهُمْ صَـٰلِحًۭا ۗ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ قَدْ جَآءَتْكُم بَيِّنَةٌۭ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ هَـٰذِهِۦ نَاقَةُ ٱللَّهِ لَكُمْ ءَايَةًۭ ۖ فَذَرُوهَا تَأْكُلْ فِىٓ أَرْضِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَلَا تَمَسُّوهَا بِسُوٓءٍۢ فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: And to the Thamūd [We sent] their brother Ṣāliḥ. He said, "O my people, worship Allāh; you have no deity other than Him. There has come to you clear evidence from your Lord. This is the she-camel of Allāh [sent] to you as a sign. So leave her to eat within Allāh's land and do not touch her with harm, lest there seize you a painful punishment.

harf.hint: A celestial body that guides travelers in the darkness; also a prostration.
harf.verse: 53:1 — By the star when it descends,

deduction.title: The Voice of Iron and Psalms
deduction.intro: A divinely-appointed leader was granted immense wisdom and a unique gift that echoed through the mountains. This individual's story is one of strength, justice, and profound devotion, blending the might of a ruler with the humility of a servant. Can you identify this remarkable figure?
clues: The mountains were commanded to echo my praise, and the birds would gather to join my chorus.
I was given a sacred book, a guide filled with psalms of devotion.
Allah made a hard metal pliable in my hands, a special gift for me to use.
From this softened metal, I fashioned protective coats of mail, linking the rings with precision.
In a decisive battle for my people, I faced and defeated a towering, tyrannical warrior.
My kingdom and prophethood were later inherited by my son, who was also blessed with incredible wisdom.

trial.label: What was my defining confrontation?
options: A contest with the Pharaoh's magicians
Building a great ark before a flood
Being thrown into a blazing fire

location.label: What was I given mastery over?

outcome.label: What was a major blessing I received?
options: Receiving the Tablets of the Law
Being raised to the heavens alive

deduction.arabic: ۞ وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا دَاوُۥدَ مِنَّا فَضْلًۭا ۖ يَـٰجِبَالُ أَوِّبِى مَعَهُۥ وَٱلطَّيْرَ ۖ وَأَلَنَّا لَهُ ٱلْحَدِيدَ
deduction.verse: And We certainly gave David from Us bounty. [We said], "O mountains, repeat [Our] praises with him, and the birds [as well]." And We made pliable for him iron, (34:10)

translations: Both of them said Our Lord we have wronged
and have mercy (on) us surely, we will be
scramble.hint: The supplication of our first parents, Adam and Eve, after their slip.
scramble.arabic: قَالَا رَبَّنَا ظَلَمْنَآ أَنفُسَنَا وَإِن لَّمْ تَغْفِرْ لَنَا وَتَرْحَمْنَا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ
scramble.verseEn: They said, "Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves, and if You do not forgive us and have mercy upon us, we will surely be among the losers."

verse.arabic_text: وَعِندَهُۥ مَفَاتِحُ ٱلْغَيْبِ لَا يَعْلَمُهَآ إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا فِى ٱلْبَرِّ وَٱلْبَحْرِ ۚ وَمَا تَسْقُطُ مِن وَرَقَةٍ إِلَّا يَعْلَمُهَا وَلَا حَبَّةٍۢ فِى ظُلُمَـٰتِ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا رَطْبٍۢ وَلَا يَابِسٍ إِلَّا فِى كِتَـٰبٍۢ مُّبِينٍۢ
verse.translation: And with Him are the keys of the unseen; none knows them except Him. And He knows what is on the land and in the sea. Not a leaf falls but that He knows it. And no grain is there within the darknesses of the earth and no moist or dry [thing] but that it is [written] in a clear record.

theme_question.correct: The limitlessness of Allah's knowledge, encompassing every detail of the seen and unseen worlds.
options: The importance of seeking knowledge from righteous scholars.
The limitlessness of Allah's knowledge, encompassing every detail of the seen and unseen worlds.
The specific rewards reserved for those who believe in the unseen.
A prohibition against trying to predict the future through divination.

educational_notes.verse_context: This verse, from Surah Al-An'am, is a powerful declaration of Allah's absolute omniscience. It appears in a section dedicated to establishing the core principles of Tawhid (monotheism) and refuting polytheism. By highlighting that Allah alone holds the 'keys to the unseen' (Mafatih al-Ghayb), the verse contrasts the limited knowledge of creation with the infinite, all-encompassing knowledge of the Creator.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The central theme is the perfection and totality of Allah's knowledge. The verse uses profound imagery to convey this concept. 'Keys of the unseen' refers to knowledge exclusively held by Allah, such as the time of the Day of Judgment or the future. The verse then moves from the abstract unseen to the tangible seen, using examples like a falling leaf, a hidden grain, or anything 'moist or dry' to illustrate that nothing, no matter how insignificant or concealed, escapes His awareness. This is meant to inspire awe, humility, and complete trust in Allah's divine wisdom and plan.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah Al-An'am (The Cattle) is the 6th chapter of the Quran and is a Makkan surah, revealed before the Hijrah. Its primary focus is on 'Aqidah (creed), specifically the oneness of Allah (Tawhid) and the rejection of shirk (polytheism). It presents logical arguments against idol worship, drawing evidence from the natural world—the creation of heavens and earth, the alternation of night and day, and the growth of vegetation—as signs of a single, all-powerful Creator. The surah also discusses prophethood, divine revelation, and the reality of the Hereafter.
